AvailableTimePolicy.description:
How the first real availability time of a record is derived (doc 12 §5.2).

``fixed_delay`` requires a positive bounded delay; all other rules must send
``delay=null`` so a stale prior delay never affects the engine.
AvailableTimePolicy.title: AvailableTimePolicy

CreationMode.description:
How a candidate is produced from the request (doc 06 §4.2).

Translate/Repair/Review require ``source_kind=code``; Generate requires
``source_kind=description``. Review alone never publishes a package.
CreationMode.title: CreationMode

EventTimeSemantics.description: How a record's event timestamp is interpreted (doc 12 §5.2).
EventTimeSemantics.title: EventTimeSemantics

MarketDataType.description:
The three accepted market-data shapes. Choice selects the target schema
and the type-specific validators (doc 11 §).
MarketDataType.title: MarketDataType

ResearchCategory.description:
Built-in research data categories (doc 12 §5.1). The set is *extensible*:
``OTHER_CUSTOM`` carries a free-text ``custom_category`` and the persisted
``category_key`` is never a closed enum at the column level (stored as text).
ResearchCategory.title: ResearchCategory

ResearchTimezoneMode.description:
Declared source timezone semantics (doc 12 §5.2). ``custom`` requires a
valid IANA identifier.
ResearchTimezoneMode.title: ResearchTimezoneMode

ResolverTrustState.description:
Registry trust selection state for a resolver (doc 09 §11.2).

``candidate``: proposed/under review, not selectable by Pre-Check.
``trusted_active``: the exact revision selectable for new conversions.
``deprecated``: removed from default new selection; historical pins still read.
``unavailable``: not resolvable (e.g. soft-deleted / withdrawn registry entry).
ResolverTrustState.title: ResolverTrustState

Role.description: Fixed, title-based human roles. No per-action grants.
Role.title: Role

RuntimeAdapter.description:
Target runtime adapters an ESP revision may be executed under (doc 09
§4.1 RuntimeAdapterRef, §4.2 "Runtime adapter"). The public semantic
contract is separate from the implementation adapter; an incompatible
adapter is never silently substituted.
RuntimeAdapter.title: RuntimeAdapter

SourceLanguage.description:
Supplied-code language (doc 06 §4.2). Required only for ``source_kind=code``.

Natural Language is NOT a source language — it maps to Generate From
Description with ``source_language=null`` (doc 06 §4.2, §14.4). ``other``
requires an ``other_language_label``.
SourceLanguage.title: SourceLanguage

TimezoneMode.description: Declared source timezone semantics. ``custom`` requires an IANA id.
TimezoneMode.title: TimezoneMode

UsageScope.description:
What system behavior a revision may feed (doc 12 §5.3, §9.3 matrix).

* ``research_backtest`` — eligible for Evidence Bundles after approval.
* ``agent_research_only`` — investigation/context only; forbidden in backtest
  evidence bundles, feature input and trade triggers.
* ``feature_input_only`` — requires an approved versioned feature definition
  before Strategy consumption; raw direct binding is forbidden.
UsageScope.title: UsageScope

VisibilityScope.description: Visibility facet — orthogonal to lifecycle and approval (CR-04).
VisibilityScope.title: VisibilityScope

post.description:
Operator recovery (INF-03, doc 20 §6): re-dispatch durable ``data``-queue
jobs still QUEUED past the redeliver grace window. The multi-actor ``data``
queue is not auto-redelivered by the scheduler — this explicit Admin action
routes each stuck job back to its actor via the payload ``job_kind``.
``grace_seconds`` defaults to the configured window; ``0`` sweeps every QUEUED
data job.

get.description:
Anonymous, read-only first-Admin onboarding signal (booleans only). Like
sign up / login it is an unauthenticated entry surface — the first Admin is
not yet authenticated — and returns no PII, so exposing it carries no more
than the deployment fact "does an Admin exist yet".

get.description:
Read-only immutable transition timeline for one capability (doc 22 §9,
§13). Any authenticated principal may read registry provenance; the POST on
this same path appends a transition and stays Admin-only.

delete.description:
Owner-or-Admin Move to Trash (doc 08 §7, §8.4). OCC token is the root
``row_version`` carried as the ``If-Match "rv-N"`` ETag (the detail GET returns
it); a stale head -> 409, a repeat delete is an idempotent no-op. Restore stays
the Admin-only Trash surface.

schema.title: Response Get Library Package Api V1 Library  Entity Id  Get

post.description:
Owner-or-Admin: ``active -> deprecated`` (doc 08 §7 "Deprecate"). No OCC — a
deprecate appends no revision, so it cannot race the head (mirrors the sibling
``market_data`` deprecate); a non-active/soft-deleted root -> 409 LIFECYCLE_BLOCKED.

Owner-or-Admin soft delete (doc 11 §10.1, Flow F). The Market Data page has
no delete control (doc 11 §2.1); this is the domain/Agent-Gateway surface.
